idea: Natural gas futures volatility spike from weather extremes

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature anomalies (5-day rolling avg deviation from 30-year baseline) plus Yahoo Finance UNG daily close via price_only adapter

behavior: Extreme temperature swings (heating/cooling demand shocks) drive natural gas price spikes. When daily temp anomaly exceeds ±2 standard deviations AND UNG volume surges 40% above 20-day average, supply anxiety triggers bullish energy positioning.

market_impact.rationale: Natural gas is a primary energy input; supply stress immediately lifts integrated energy and downstream utility hedges.

trade_logic.entry: When 5-day rolling temp anomaly exceeds ±2σ AND UNG volume > 1.4× 20-day MA AND UNG closes up >2% on the day
trade_logic.exit: After 7 calendar days OR when temp anomaly returns to within ±1σ for 3 consecutive days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal weather swings occur reliably; temperature anomalies cross 2σ multiple times per quarter in most climates.

idea: EV charger density explosion in affluent zip codes

data.source: OpenChargeMap API weekly charger count deltas in top 20 affluent metro areas (zip code filtering) vs Yahoo Finance XLY weekly price via price_only adapter

behavior: Rapid charger deployment in wealthy neighborhoods signals credible EV adoption acceleration and consumer confidence in clean tech. Weekly net new charger installations >50 units in top 5 metros correlate with discretionary spending strength.

market_impact.rationale: Charger buildout in affluent areas is a leading indicator of high-income consumer willingness to invest in EV infrastructure; strong EV sales follow deployment surges.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net charger additions in top 5 metros exceed 50 units AND XLY closes > 5-day SMA AND RSI(14) < 70
trade_logic.exit: After 21 calendar days OR if weekly charger additions fall below 30 units for 2 consecutive weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Infrastructure investment in affluent areas happens in seasonal waves; deployment surges typically occur in spring and fall renewal cycles.

idea: Mortgage rate shock yield-curve steepening hedge

data.source: FRED series DGS10 (10-year yield) and DGS2 (2-year yield) daily via fred_series adapter, plus XLU (utilities ETF) daily close via price_only adapter

behavior: When 2-10 year spread widens >25 bps in a single week after being inverted or flat, bond market is pricing in rate-cut expectations. Utilities and REITs rally on lower discount rates.

market_impact.rationale: Steeper curves and falling long rates compress the required return on stable utility cash flows, driving re-rating upward.

trade_logic.entry: If (DGS10 - DGS2) increases by >25 bps over 5 trading days AND XLU closes above 20-day SMA AND 10-year yield drops >10 bps from 5-day high
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if spread compresses <15 bps from entry level

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Yield curve reshapes occur monthly in response to Fed messaging, inflation data, and employment reports; steepening events fire reliably.

idea: Grocery demand surge from layoff chatter

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'layoffs near me' and 'how to apply for unemployment' via google_trends adapter, plus XLP (consumer staples) weekly close via price_only adapter

behavior: Spikes in job-loss-related searches precede stress-buying of shelf-stable foods and household essentials. Staples retailers see margin expansion and volume lift as households shift spending from discretionary to staples.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer staples outperform when households face job anxiety; defensive rotation into grocery/essentials is immediate and predictable.

trade_logic.entry: If Google Trends 'layoffs near me' + 'unemployment application' combined score rises >40% week-over-week AND XLP RSI(14) < 65 AND XLP closes > open
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days OR if layoff search trend falls >30% from peak

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Quarterly earnings cuts and macro disappointments trigger layoff announcements and chatter; each cycle fires the signal 2–3 times.

idea: Airline load factor compression from weak business travel

data.source: BTS airline load factor (weekly average via bts_airline_load_factor adapter) plus XLY (consumer discretionary) weekly close via price_only adapter

behavior: Load factors fall when business travel weakens (corporate cost-cutting, recession fear). Falling load factors below 80% compress airline pricing power, which then ripples into hospitality and leisure discretionary weakness.

market_impact.rationale: Load factor compression signals demand destruction in high-margin business travel; airlines cut capacity and pricing, hotels face lower occupancy.

trade_logic.entry: If BTS weekly load factor drops >2 percentage points in a single week AND load factor < 81% AND XLY closes below 50-day SMA
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days OR if load factor recovers above 82% for 2 consecutive weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal business travel cycles and quarterly earnings cuts trigger load factor drops every 6–8 weeks.

idea: Freight rail carload plunge from manufacturing slowdown

data.source: FRED series RAILFRTCG (railroad freight carloads, weekly) via fred_series adapter, plus XLI (industrials) weekly close via price_only adapter

behavior: Carload volumes fall sharply when manufacturing activity weakens. A >5% drop in weekly carloads vs 4-week MA signals demand destruction; industrials and materials underperform within 2 weeks.

market_impact.rationale: Rail carload data is a real-time proxy for industrial production; sharp declines precede earnings misses and guidance cuts.

trade_logic.entry: If RAILFRTCG weekly < 4-week MA × 0.95 AND XLI closes below 20-day SMA AND XLI RSI(14) < 50
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if RAILFRTCG recovers to 4-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Quarterly inventory corrections and seasonal demand shifts trigger carload drops reliably; signal fires every 4–6 weeks.

idea: Healthcare cost panic from medical billing scandal news surge

data.source: RSS feed article count from healthcare/medical billing news sites (healthcare.gov, CMS press releases, major medical journals) via rss_count adapter, plus XLV (healthcare) weekly close via price_only adapter

behavior: Spikes in news coverage of medical billing errors, surprise hospital bills, or insurance denials trigger consumer anxiety. Healthcare stocks dip on regulatory scrutiny fear, then recover as overshooting subsides.

market_impact.rationale: News surges on billing scandals create temporary fear of margin compression and regulatory action, causing tactical healthcare sector weakness.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS article count from healthcare billing topics exceeds 200% of 4-week MA AND XLV closes < 20-day SMA AND article count rises >50% week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: After 7 calendar days OR if article count falls below 120% of 4-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Medical billing scandals emerge periodically; each major news cycle (Congressional hearings, investigative reports) fires the signal.

idea: Seismic activity construction insurance premium spike

data.source: USGS earthquake activity daily API (magnitude >3.0 events in construction-heavy regions: CA, WA, OK) via earthquake_activity adapter, plus XLB (materials/construction) weekly close via price_only adapter

behavior: Clusters of >3.0 magnitude earthquakes in major metro areas spike construction insurance demand and delay project starts. Materials demand softens as contractors postpone work, but insurance/reinsurance stocks see margin improvement.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake swarms delay construction spending and postpone materials purchases; materials producers face near-term demand destruction.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ earthquakes >3.0 magnitude occur within a 7-day window in CA/WA/OK AND XLB closes < 10-day SMA AND XLB shows negative momentum (close < open for 2+ of last 3 days)
trade_logic.exit: After 14 calendar days OR if no magnitude >3.0 events occur for 5 consecutive days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity clusters occur 2–3 times per quarter in active regions; each swarm triggers construction caution.

idea: Energy-intensive manufacturing pivot away from peak-hours (EIA demand spike fade)

data.source: EIA daily electricity demand (peak hour demand via eia_electricity adapter) plus XLI (industrials) and XLU (utilities) daily closes via price_only adapter

behavior: When peak-hour electricity demand spikes >3% above rolling 20-day average on same day, it signals industrial production surge. Industrials rally, utilities face margin pressure. Fade occurs as demand normalization follows within 2–3 weeks.

market_impact.rationale: Peak electricity demand spikes are a real-time read on factory utilization; rallies in industrial output drive equipment/machinery demand.

trade_logic.entry: If EIA peak-hour demand > 20-day MA × 1.03 AND XLI closes > 5-day SMA AND XLI volume > 10-day MA
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if EIA demand falls below 20-day MA for 3 consecutive days

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Industrial production cycles and seasonal demand spikes trigger electricity surges every 2–3 weeks; signal fires reliably.
